titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/219

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=489 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.048048+00 | translation moa=490 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.028862+00

p.cl: Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris . Philippus filius Ph’ . De Pethcox Salutem

Sciatis me concessisse et hac mea Carta
Confirmasse Deo et Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros . et Monachis ibidem Deo seruientibus . pro animabus Dominorum meorum Cospatrici .
et Waldeui . Comitum De Dunbar et pro animabus patris et Matris mee . et Omnium antecessorum meorum . et Pro salute domini mei Comitis
Patricii . et pro salute mea . et omnium successorum meorum terras illas in territorio De Pethcox . quas pater meus eisdem Monach’ dedit . scilicet .
terram illam que Iacet inter diuisas terre Monachorum de Hertesheuede . et Riuulum qui nominatur prestmunethburne . quam
predicti Monachi ex donatione . et precepti patris mei per predictum Riuulum . terre sue De Hertesheuede . incluserunt . et Decem acras In
Cultura De Beleside . terre sue de Edmunest’ propinquius adiacentes . In liberam . puram . et perpetuam Elemosinam .
Quare uolo . et concedo ut predicti Monachi De Melros prenominatas terras . habeant . teneant . et possideant . Ita libere . et quie
te . Plene et Honorifice . sicut aliquam Elemosinam liberius . Quietius Plenius et honorificentius . tenent et possident .
Et ego et heredes mei Manutenebimus et warantizabimus predictas terras prefatis Monachis contra omnes homines inperpetuum .
et adquietabimus de omni terreno seruicio et consuetudine . et exactione seculari .

Test’ . Comiti Patricio Domino meo . Patricio filio eius
Bernardo fraser . Rogero . De Merlay . Adam de Paulew’ . Adam De Gord’ . Thom’ De Neseb’. [Philippo filio meo et struck through] Waldeuo
persona . de withnham . Magistro Willelmo de hercheld’. Magistro Iohanne persona . Et Multis Aliis .

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Philip son of Philip of Pitcox sends greeting. Know me to have allowed, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks in that place serving God, for the souls of my lords, Cospatric and Waldef, earls of Dunbar, and for the souls of my father and mother, and of all my ancestors, and for the salvation of my lord, Earl Patrick, and for my own salvation, and of all my successors, the lands in the territory of Pitcox which my father has given to the same monks, that is, the land which lies between the boundaries of the monks' land at Hartside and the little stream which is called Pressmennan Burn, which the aforesaid monks have annexed along the aforesaid stream to their own land at Hartside by reason of the donation and instruction of my father, and ten acres in ploughland at 'Beleside' most closely adjacent to their land at Edmundstoun; in free, pure, and perpetual alms. Wherefore I will and allow that the aforesaid monks of Melrose should have, hold, and possess the aforenamed lands, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably, as they freely, quitely, fully and honourably hold and possess any alms. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant the aforesaid lands to the aforementioned monks against all men for ever, and we shall discharge all earthly service and custom, and secular exaction. As witnesses: Earl Patrick, my lord; Patrick his son; Bernard Fraser; Roger de Merlay; Adam of Palworth; Adam of Gordon; Thomas of Nisbet [Philip my son and struck through] Waldef parson of Whittinghame; Master William of Earlston; Master John the parson; and many others.
